name: Coll de les Ventoses and Coll d'Arca – circular from Tarragona
name_original: Coll de les Ventoses y Coll d'Arca – circular desde Tarragona
region_intro: Road cycling around Tarragones offers diverse landscapes, transitioning from coastal paths along the Mediterranean to rugged mountainous areas inland. The region features impressive mountain ranges such as the Prades Mountains and Montsant Natural Park, providing varied terrain. Cyclists can also explore fertile lands characterized by extensive vineyards and olive groves. This varied geography ensures a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Tarragones The most popular road cycling route is Castell d'Escornalbou and Riudecanyes Reservoir Loop, a 40.8 miles (65.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This moderate route features scenic views of the reservoir and historical sites. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coll de les Ventoses and Coll d'Arca – circular from Tarragona, a difficult 58.5 miles (94.1 km) path. It includes significant elevation gains through the region's challenging mountain passes. Local road cyclists also love the Tarragona - Reus - Cambrils - Salou – circular through the Campo de Tarragona, a 44.6 miles (71.7 km) trail leading through coastal towns and agricultural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Road cycling around Tarragones is defined by coastal stretches, challenging mountain passes, and picturesque routes through vineyards and olive groves. The extensive road network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult climbs. The routes in Tarragones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 370 reviews. More than 17,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tarragones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Tarragones?
A: The Tarragones region offers a wide array of road cycling routes, with over 260 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Tarragones?
A: Road cycling in Tarragones features incredibly diverse terrain. You can expect scenic coastal paths along the Mediterranean, rugged mountainous areas inland like the Prades Mountains and Montsant Natural Park, and fertile lands characterized by extensive vineyards and olive groves. This variety ensures a rich and engaging cycling experience.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Tarragones offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 63 easy routes, often found along the flatter coastal areas or through agricultural landscapes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38290978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Tower of Cambrils – End of the Asphalt loop from Salou - Port Aventura&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option.

Q: What are some of the most challenging road cycling climbs in the Tarragones region?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Tarragones boasts several significant climbs. Notable ascents include La Mussara, Coll de la Teixeta, and Mont Caro, which is an Hors category climb. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964442906/coll-de-les-ventoses-and-coll-d-arca-circular-from-tarragona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de les Ventoses and Coll d'Arca – circular from Tarragona&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38295955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riudecanyes Reservoir – Colldejou Climb loop from Salou - Port Aventura&lt;/a&gt; feature substantial elevation gains.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Tarragones?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Tarragones are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970489483/castell-d-escornalbou-and-riudecanyes-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell d'Escornalbou and Riudecanyes Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970526346/tarragona-reus-cambrils-salou-circular-through-the-campo-de-tarragona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarragona - Reus - Cambrils - Salou – circular through the Campo de Tarragona&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What scenic views or natural features can I expect on road cycling routes in Tarragones?
A: Road cycling in Tarragones offers breathtaking scenery, from golden sandy beaches and dramatic coastal cliffs to the rugged peaks of the Prades Mountains and Montsant Natural Park. You'll also cycle through tranquil olive groves and extensive vineyards, particularly in the Priorat and Montsant wine regions. The Ebro Delta to the south provides a unique landscape of wetlands and rice fields.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or cultural sites I can visit while road cycling in Tarragones?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history and culture. You can cycle past ancient Roman structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2646467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarragona Roman Amphitheatre&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/463399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Ferreres Aqueduct (Devil's Bridge)&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sites include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/841251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tamarit Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3909837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Tarragona&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like the 'Olive Oil Route', even integrate cultural stops at local cooperatives and mills.

Q: Which routes offer the best coastal views?
A: For stunning coastal views, consider routes that connect the coastal towns. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970526346/tarragona-reus-cambrils-salou-circular-through-the-campo-de-tarragona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarragona - Reus - Cambrils - Salou – circular through the Campo de Tarragona&lt;/a&gt; route offers beautiful stretches along the sea. Many routes originating from Salou or Cambrils also provide excellent opportunities to enjoy the Mediterranean coastline.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Tarragones?
A: Tarragones generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and less crowded roads, making them ideal. Even in winter, the mild Mediterranean climate can be suitable for cycling, especially along the coast. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during peak season.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Tarragones?
A: Yes, the region includes family-friendly road cycling routes, particularly those connecting coastal towns like Cambrils, Salou, and La Pineda. These routes often feature flatter terrain and dedicated paths, providing a safe and enjoyable experience for families.

Q: What kind of cycling infrastructure and services are available in the Tarragones region?
A: The Tarragones region is well-equipped for cyclists, with many 'Bike-friendly' areas providing amenities for rest and equipment storage. Bike rental services are readily available, especially in towns like Cambrils. Specialized agencies also organize events and offer services for cyclists of all levels, ensuring a supportive environment for your road cycling adventures.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that pass through vineyards or olive groves?
A: Yes, the fertile lands of Tarragones are characterized by extensive vineyards and olive groves, particularly in the Priorat and Montsant wine regions, and the Baix Camp area. Many routes, such as those inspired by the 'Olive Oil Route', will take you through these picturesque agricultural landscapes, offering a unique cultural and scenic experience.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about their experience in Tarragones?
A: The road cycling routes in Tarragones are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 370 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's diverse landscapes, the variety of routes catering to all abilities, and the stunning views, from coastal stretches to challenging mountain passes.

This section coincides with a large part of the climb to Coll de les Ventoses or Alt de la Talaia. It is a beautiful and highly recommended section.

The climb to Les Ventoses is one of the most popular in the area, with its seven kilometers and a very constant average gradient of 4.5%, which never goes beyond 6%. It is always frequented by cyclists and has a low level of road traffic. The road is perfectly paved and as it goes up, it allows you to contemplate a beautiful panoramic view of the Penedès plain and even the Mediterranean Sea, thanks to its numerous horseshoe curves.

This port has two slopes, Juncosa del Montmell and Aiguaviva. Both are hard, with percentages of more than 10%, but the one that comes from Juncosa is the hardest thanks to its hard final section of 17%, with two horseshoe curves in a row that do not allow any rest. It allows to connect both the Bisbal del Penedès and Rodonyà with Aiguaviva and the road from Sant Jaume dels Domenys to the Plana de Manlleu.
